role: A registry-aware embedder-layer picker chip that lets the user choose which embedding layer (e.g. default, pplx) to view data through.
capabilities: Fetches available layers from /api/layers on mount
Computes the union of layers across spaces and sorts them
Hides itself when only one layer exists
Displays the current layer with a toggle button
Opens an animated dropdown menu of layer options
Sets the selected embedding (null for default) via setEmb prop
Closes menu on scrim click or option selection
Applies semantic stamp data attributes for UI instrumentation
Gracefully ignores a missing layers endpoint
substance_why: The component implements the full lifecycle: data fetching, state management, menu interaction, selection mapping, and conditional visibility, with explicit handling for the default layer and missing endpoint.
interactions_present: Click chip button to toggle menu open/closed
Click a layer option to select it and close the menu
interactions_missing: No keyboard navigation inside the menu (arrow keys, Home/End)
No Escape key handler to close the menu
No focus trap or focus return to the trigger
No Enter/Space selection semantics on menu items beyond native button
No loading, error, or empty-state feedback for the layers fetch
No retry mechanism if fetch fails
No screen-reader announcement on layer change
performance_risks: fetchLayers is called on every component mount with no caching, SWR, or global store
Layer union and sort run on every fetch but are not memoized across renders
Menu items are re-rendered on every parent render without memoization
Framer-motion dropdown animates scale/opacity which can cause compositing work on many mounts
reuse_why: Keep the registry-true data model, default/null mapping, and hide-if-single-layer behavior, but rebuild the UI around shared menu primitives and a proper data-fetching hook.
decomposition_hints: Extract a useLayers hook for fetching, unioning, and caching /api/layers
Extract a generic ChipMenu or Dropdown primitive from useChipMenu and the motion menu markup
Separate the layer-option item rendering into a presentational LayerOption component
Move stamp path generation into a small helper or shared instrumentation utility
